title: Aglaia aphanamixis
intro: Aglaia aphanamixis, also known as the buah keras or balau tree, is a species of plant in the family Meliaceae, commonly found in the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The health benefits of Aglaia aphanamixis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Aglaia aphanamixis is used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments to treat a range of health issues, including fever, wounds, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Aglaia aphanamixis is a species of tree in the family Meliaceae. It is a part of the genus Aglaia, which consists of trees native to tropical Asia. The genus Aglaia is further classified under the subfamily Melioideae, and the family Meliaceae is part of the order Sapindales. This species is native to Southeast Asia, particularly in countries such as Malaysia and Indonesia. The tree is also known as the 'Buddha's head' due to its unique fruit shape.
uses: Aglaia aphanamixis, also known as the Buah keras tree, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant's bark, leaves, and seeds are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The extracts from the plant have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. In traditional medicine, Aglaia aphanamixis is used to treat conditions such as dysentery and diarrhea. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and potential applications.
benefits: Aglaia aphanamixis, also known as the Borneo ironwood, has been studied for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory properties and its ability to protect against oxidative stress. side_effects: Aglaia aphanamixis, also known as the Yellow Bute tree, may cause various side effects due to its toxicity. The plant contains toxic compounds such as limonoids, which can be harmful if ingested in large quantities.
